zh.Borstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel於1190年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親William FitzNeale40歲，母親Mrs Alice FitzNeale25歲。他於1250年之前與Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.Borstall, Buckinghamshire, England: 英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾波阿斯塔爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel于1190年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，他的父亲William FitzNeale40岁，母亲Mrs Alice FitzNeale25岁。他于1250年之前与Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: (i) relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + roy ‘king’, ‘son of the king’. It was often taken to imply that the original bearer was a bastard son of the king ( compare Fillary ). (ii) relationship name ‘son of Ruadh’, a Gaelic nickname meaning ‘red’, is also possible.

nameMeanings.name: Maurice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Armand, Denys, Gabrielle, Antoine, Benoit, Camille, Cecile, Francois, Gaston, Germain.
French, English, Welsh, Scottish, and Irish: from the Old French personal name Maurice, Latin Mauritius (see Morris ).

variants.name: Morisseau
meanings: French: derivative of the personal name Morisse, a variant of Maurice .

variants.name: Morris
meanings: English and Scottish: from Maurice, an Old French personal name introduced to Britain by the Normans. It is derived from Latin Mauritius, a derivative of Maurus (see Moore ). This was the name of several early Christian saints. In some cases it may be a nickname of the same derivation for someone with a swarthy complexion.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Muirghis, a variant of Ó Muirgheasa (see Morrissey ).

Welsh: Anglicized form of the personal name Meurig (from Latin Mauritius), which was gradually superseded in Wales by Morus, Morys, a derivative of the Anglo-Norman French form of the name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Morisset
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Jacques, Pierre, Camille, Edouard, Jean-Guy, Ulysse, Yvon.
French: derivative of the personal name Morisse, a variant of Maurice . Compare Morissette .

variants.name: Morice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Raul, Carlos, Jorge, Leticia, Rafael.
French: variant of   Maurice   (see   Morris  ).
Scottish (Aberdeenshire): variant of   Morris  .

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: Norman, English, Irish: (i) relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + roy ‘king’, ‘son of the king’. It was often taken to imply that the original bearer was a bastard son of the king ( compare Fillary ). (ii) relationship name ‘son of Ruadh’, a Gaelic nickname meaning ‘red’, is also possible.

nameMeanings.name: Niel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Conley.
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Neill  .
English: variant of   Neal  .

variants.name: Neill
meanings: Irish and Scottish: variant of   Neil  , also probably representing shortened forms of the derived patronymics   McNeill   or the comparable Irish   O'Neill  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel was born in 1190, in Buckinghamshire, England, his father, William FitzNeale, was 40 and his mother, Mrs Alice FitzNeale, was 25. He married Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel before 1250, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel est né en 1190, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, William FitzNeale, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Mrs Alice FitzNeale, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel avant 1250, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel 1190 in Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William FitzNeale 40 und seine Mutter Mrs Alice FitzNeale 25. Er hat vor 1250 in England Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel è nato il nel 1190 nel Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, William FitzNeale, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Alice FitzNeale, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel prima del 1250, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Borstall, Buckinghamshire, England: イギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアボアルスタル
ja.Borstall,Buckinghamshire, England: イギリスブクインガムシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Nielが1190年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父William FitzNealeは40歳，母Mrs Alice FitzNealeは25歳であった。1250年以前にイギリスでMrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Nielと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel(이)가 1190년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William FitzNeale(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Alice FitzNeale(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1250년 이전 영국에서 Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

mn.Borstall, Buckinghamshire, England: Boarstall, Buccingahamscir, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel nasceu em 1190, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William FitzNeale, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Alice FitzNeale, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel antes 1250, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel родился в 1190 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William FitzNeale было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs Alice FitzNeale, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel перед 1250 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel nació en 1190, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William FitzNeale, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Mrs Alice FitzNeale, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Mrs Thomas Fitz Maurice Fitz Niel antes de 1250, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
